payload.description: Implemented LLM-based Semgrep rule generation at promotion time: RuleGenerator class, check_override param in promote_to_spec, --generate-check CLI flag, 42 new tests (66 total in targeted files), all passing
Created e2e test suite (6 tests) for promote --generate-check pipeline; verified live LLM call produces valid Semgrep rule

payload.decision: RuleGenerator follows same async context manager + httpx pattern as LLMExtractor; imports shared provider config (LLMProvider, API_ENDPOINTS, DEFAULT_MODELS) from llm.py rather than duplicating
payload.rationale: Consistent patterns reduce cognitive load; single source of truth for provider config
alternatives_rejected: Duplicating provider config in rule_generator.py; using a shared base class (over-engineering for 2 consumers)

payload.decision: promote_to_spec gets check_override param (not RuleGenerator integration); CLI wires them together
payload.rationale: Keeps promotion pure/sync, generation async/optional; backward-compatible; testable independently
alternatives_rejected: Embedding RuleGenerator call inside promote_to_spec; making promote_to_spec async

payload.summary: RuleGenerator in src/sine/rule_generator.py generates RuleCheck from ValidatedPattern via LLM. Prompt includes all 5 check type schemas. _parse_check validates against Pydantic models with extra=forbid. CLI --generate-check flag on promote command wires it up. Graceful fallback to placeholder on any failure.

payload.decision: DocsAgent reuses _filter_patterns and _deduplicate_patterns by copying the small methods from ArchitectureAgent rather than extracting a shared helper
payload.rationale: Methods are ~20 lines each; extracting a shared helper adds complexity for 2 consumers. Copy is fine until a third agent appears.
alternatives_rejected: Extract shared base class or mixin for filter/dedup logic

payload.decision: DocsAgent._parse_sections uses simple regex line-by-line splitting on heading markers (# ## ###) rather than a markdown parsing library
payload.rationale: No new dependencies; stdlib-only; handles code block preservation adequately for architecture docs; full markdown AST parsing (e.g. markdown-it) is overkill for heading extraction
alternatives_rejected: Use markdown-it-py or mistune for proper AST parsing

payload.decision: CLI --docs flag creates DocsAgent in separate branch of _run_research, skipping WebSearchClient entirely
payload.rationale: DocsAgent reads local files only — no web search needed; keeping separate branches avoids unnecessary WebSearchClient initialization and scorer setup
alternatives_rejected: Always create WebSearchClient and pass None to DocsAgent; make DocsAgent accept optional search_client

payload.summary: DocsAgent._find_doc_files searches for markdown docs in priority order: ARCHITECTURE.md > DESIGN.md > docs/architecture/**/*.md + docs/design/**/*.md > docs/adr/**/*.md + adr/**/*.md > CONTRIBUTING.md. Excludes node_modules, .git, venv, __pycache__, build, dist, .venv, .tox. If given a file path instead of directory, returns just that file.

payload.description: Implemented DocsAgent: new agent that discovers patterns from project documentation (ARCHITECTURE.md, DESIGN.md, ADRs). Created docs.py with DocSection dataclass, markdown parser, file discovery. Added --docs flag to sine research CLI. 23 unit tests + 3 e2e tests, 381 total tests pass, ruff/mypy clean. Updated README.md with discovery pipeline docs.

payload.decision: Replaced DuckDuckGo HTML scraping with Brave Search API via pluggable SearchProvider protocol
payload.rationale: DDG scraping was brittle (CSS selectors break); Brave has free tier (2k queries/month); Protocol pattern enables future providers
alternatives_rejected: Keep DuckDuckGo; use Google Custom Search (requires billing)

payload.decision: retry_async utility with exponential backoff + jitter used by both BraveSearchProvider and _fetch_page_text
payload.rationale: Shared retry logic avoids duplication; jitter prevents thundering herd; configurable retryable_exceptions keeps it flexible
alternatives_rejected: Per-call inline retry loops; tenacity library (unnecessary dependency)

payload.decision: Three-tier search provider fallback: Google > Brave > DuckDuckGo, selected at runtime via env vars
payload.rationale: Google is most reliable for power users; Brave has free 2k/month tier; DDG HTML scraping works with zero config for first-time users. All implement SearchProvider Protocol for uniform delegation.
alternatives_rejected: Require API key (blocks first-time users); auto-detect at import time (prevents lazy loading)

payload.decision: GeneratedCheck dataclass wraps RuleCheck + scaffolding; scaffolding extracted from LLM JSON before Pydantic validation
payload.rationale: Pydantic models use extra=forbid, so scaffolding key must be popped before model_validate. Keeping scaffolding separate from the check preserves clean model boundaries.
alternatives_rejected: Add scaffolding field to RuleCheck models (violates Semgrep schema); post-process YAML output with regex

payload.decision: Scaffolding written as YAML comments (# Requirement: ...) above rule body in save_spec, not embedded in the rule data
payload.rationale: Comments are visible to humans reviewing rules but ignored by Semgrep; keeps the YAML valid and parseable while providing context for the AI-generated rule
alternatives_rejected: Embed scaffolding as metadata field in the rule YAML (pollutes Semgrep config); separate .md sidecar file (extra file management)

payload.summary: SearchProvider Protocol (providers.py) defines execute_search() + close(). Three implementations: BraveSearchProvider (brave.py, X-Subscription-Token header), GoogleSearchProvider (google.py, key+cx params), DuckDuckGoSearchProvider (duckduckgo.py, HTML scraping with BeautifulSoup). WebSearchClient delegates to provider via search_provider param. CLI _create_search_provider() selects based on env vars. All providers handle allowed_domains via site: rewriting + post-filter safety net.

RuleGenerator._build_prompt requests scaffolding (Requirement/Constraints/Design) in JSON output. _parse_check pops scaffolding key before Pydantic validation, returns tuple[RuleCheck|None, str|None]. generate_check returns GeneratedCheck(check, scaffolding). CLI _generate_check returns GeneratedCheck. promote command threads scaffolding to save_spec which writes it as YAML comments.

retry_async(fn, *args, max_retries=3, base_delay=1.0, max_delay=30.0, retryable_exceptions=(OSError,)) in retry.py. Exponential backoff with full jitter (random.uniform(0, delay)). Used by architecture.py _fetch_page_text (max_retries=2, retryable_exceptions=(httpx.RequestError,)). Returns Any — callers should type-annotate the result variable.

payload.description: Full verification passed: 424 tests, ruff clean, mypy clean. Fixed pre-existing unused imports and formatting. Fixed mypy SearchProvider type annotation in _create_search_provider.

payload.decision: Renamed project from sine to lookout: package, CLI entry point, config files, default directories, class names, documentation
payload.rationale: Project rebrand. All references updated atomically: git mv for directories, sed for bulk imports, manual edits for prose/config/class names. 421 tests pass (3 pre-existing Click mix_stderr failures unchanged).
alternatives_rejected: Gradual rename with aliases (unnecessary complexity for alpha project)

payload.summary: Full project rename completed: package dir, CLI entry point, config files (.lookout-rules, .lookout-patterns, .lookout-baseline.json), class names (LookoutConfig, LookoutError), documentation, CI workflows. All 424 tests pass. No remaining sine references in source/tests/config.

payload.description: Plan pattern repository structure: rename guidelines to patterns, add language/framework hierarchy

payload.decision: Renamed guideline_id to pattern_id throughout enforcement pipeline (Finding, BaselineEntry, SARIF, semgrep extraction, runner). Added backward-compat in baseline loader for old keys.
payload.rationale: Unifies terminology between enforcement (was 'guideline') and discovery (already 'pattern') pipelines
alternatives_rejected: Keep guideline_id (inconsistent terminology)

payload.decision: Hierarchical pattern repository: PatternSpecFile (schema_version 2) with PatternSpec → LanguageVariant → {GenericVariant, FrameworkVariant}. Version constraints at pattern, language, and framework levels. Variants stored in single YAML file per pattern.
payload.rationale: Semgrep rules cannot span languages for structural patterns; each language/framework needs its own rule. Single file keeps pattern as one conceptual unit.
alternatives_rejected: Separate files per language variant (harder to version/review); generic Semgrep mode (too limited for structural patterns)

payload.decision: Schema version dispatch: v1 (RuleSpecFile) and v2 (PatternSpecFile) coexist. Loader detects schema_version field. Existing v1 YAML files require zero migration.
payload.rationale: Backward compatibility — all 7 existing rules continue to work unchanged. New patterns use v2 format incrementally.
alternatives_rejected: Force-migrate all rules to v2 (unnecessary churn for alpha project)

payload.decision: Renamed built_in_rules/ directory to patterns/. Added backward-compat aliases (get_built_in_rules_path, load_built_in_rules) in rules_loader.py.
payload.rationale: Aligns directory name with 'pattern' terminology. Aliases prevent breaking external code.
alternatives_rejected: Keep built_in_rules name (inconsistent with new pattern terminology)

payload.description: Completed pattern repository structure: guideline→pattern rename, hierarchical PatternSpec models (v2 schema), loader/compiler updates, directory rename, 3 seed patterns (DI-001, REPO-001, LAYER-001) with Python+TypeScript+framework variants. 458 tests pass, ruff/mypy/format clean.

payload.decision: Replaced AirlockClient (httpx) with LiteLLMClient (litellm) for multi-provider LLM routing
payload.rationale: litellm routes to 100+ providers via model string (e.g. gemini/gemini-3.1-pro-tools), eliminates custom HTTP client, reads API keys from env vars automatically
alternatives_rejected: Keep AirlockClient with httpx (single endpoint only); add multiple httpx clients per provider (duplication)

payload.decision: LLMClient Protocol + LLMResponse dataclass as provider abstraction layer
payload.rationale: Protocol enables future native SDK implementations without changing pipeline code; LLMResponse drops raw field (unused), keeps content/model/usage; @runtime_checkable allows isinstance checks in tests
alternatives_rejected: Abstract base class (forces inheritance); duck typing without protocol (no type safety)

payload.decision: Custom exponential backoff in LiteLLMClient instead of litellm built-in retry
payload.rationale: Set num_retries=0 on litellm.acompletion and handle retry ourselves with 2^attempt backoff. Gives control over which exceptions are retried (RateLimitError, APIConnectionError, Timeout) vs immediate failure (AuthenticationError)
alternatives_rejected: Use litellm num_retries (less control over retry behavior and error classification)

payload.decision: Import litellm exceptions from litellm.exceptions with aliases (LiteLLMRateLimitError, etc.) instead of litellm.RateLimitError
payload.rationale: litellm does not explicitly export exception classes from top-level module; mypy strict mode raises attr-defined errors. Direct import from litellm.exceptions resolves mypy and makes patch targets clear in tests
alternatives_rejected: Access via litellm.RateLimitError (mypy errors); add type: ignore comments (masks real issues)

payload.decision: ModelSelector widget uses static get_available_models() dict at init, no live API call
payload.rationale: litellm.models_by_provider is a static dict of known models per provider, no network call needed. Filtered to SUPPORTED_PROVIDERS (gemini, anthropic, openai). Cascading Select widgets: provider -> model
alternatives_rejected: Fetch models from API at runtime (slow, requires auth); hardcode model list (stale quickly)

payload.decision: ConfigEditorScreen saves to app.tui_config in-memory only (session-only), no file persistence
payload.rationale: TUIConfig is a Pydantic model on LookoutApp; saving to disk requires deciding file format and location. Session-only is sufficient for alpha; file persistence can be added later
alternatives_rejected: Write to lookout.toml (premature for alpha); write to ~/.config/lookout/tui.toml (adds complexity)

payload.summary: LLMClient Protocol (clients/protocol.py) defines async context manager + chat() -> LLMResponse. LiteLLMClient (clients/litellm_client.py) implements it using litellm.acompletion(). __aenter__/__aexit__ are no-ops (litellm manages connections). Retry: custom exponential backoff (2^attempt) on RateLimitError/APIConnectionError/Timeout; AuthenticationError fails immediately. _parse_response handles malformed responses with RuntimeError. Module-level helpers: get_available_models() filters litellm.models_by_provider to SUPPORTED_PROVIDERS; validate_model_env() delegates to litellm.validate_environment().

TUIConfig (config.py): llm_model (default gemini/gemini-3.1-pro-tools), llm_temperature (0.3), llm_max_tokens (4096), llm_timeout (120.0), llm_max_retries (3), plus prompts_dir, index_path, patterns_dir. LookoutApp (app.py): 3 screens - browser (b), generation (g), config (c). GenerationPipelineScreen creates LiteLLMClient from config lazily in _ensure_pipeline() with asyncio.Lock. ModelSelector change invalidates client+pipeline under lock. ConfigEditorScreen has ModelSelector + Input fields, saves to tui_config session-only.

ModelSelector (widgets/model_selector.py): two cascading Select widgets (provider -> model). Reads get_available_models() at init (static dict, no API call). Parses provider/model from current_model string. Posts ModelSelector.ModelChanged(model) message with full 'provider/model-name' string. on_select_changed handles both selects: provider change repopulates model list and auto-selects first; model change emits event. CSS: #model-selector and #config-model-selector dock top, height 3.

Tests mock litellm at module level: patch('lookout_tui.clients.litellm_client.litellm'). Exception classes patched individually: patch(f'{_MOD}.LiteLLMRateLimitError', err_cls) where err_cls = type('RateLimitError', (Exception,), {}). ModelSelector tests patch get_available_models with _PATCH_TARGET constant. Pipeline tests use AsyncMock for client.chat with LLMResponse fixtures. 75 tests total across 7 test files, asyncio_mode=auto.

payload.decision: Switch TUI screen navigation from letter keys (b/r/g/c) to number keys (1/2/3/4)
payload.rationale: Frees r for Tier 1 Refresh and g for screen-specific use per Monospace TUI §2.2
alternatives_rejected: Keep letter mnemonics — conflicts with Tier 1/2 standard keys

payload.decision: Create ci() helper in keys.py for case-insensitive binding pairs
payload.rationale: Monospace TUI §2.2 requires both cases bound, only lowercase shown. Separate module avoids circular imports (app.py imports screens, screens need ci)
alternatives_rejected: Define ci() in app.py — causes circular import with screens

payload.decision: Remap approve/reject from a/r to Ctrl+A/Ctrl+X on Registry and Generation screens
payload.rationale: Frees a for Tier 2 Add/Create and r for Tier 1 Refresh per §2.2 standard key semantics
alternatives_rejected: Keep a=approve, r=reject — violates Tier 1/2 key assignments

payload.decision: Add LLM config fields (llm_provider, llm_model, llm_temperature, llm_max_tokens, llm_max_retries) to LookoutConfig
payload.rationale: Core CLI commands (promote, research) hardcoded anthropic as default provider with no config file support. Now configurable from lookout.toml or pyproject.toml [tool.lookout]
alternatives_rejected: Keep LLM config TUI-only — leaves CLI users without persistence

payload.summary: Completed 7-point compliance audit: (1) number-key screen nav, (2) ci() case-insensitive bindings, (3) F-key duals (F1/F3/F5), (4) ?=Help action, (5) Tier 2 a/d/e/y semantics, (6) responsive breakpoint CSS classes via on_resize, (7) TUI-DESIGN.md updated with waivers and key maps

payload.description: Implemented batch generation system: models, grid builder, prompt builder, parser, providers (Anthropic/Gemini), orchestrator, CLI commands, TUI screen. 76 new tests, all passing. 571 total core tests passing.

payload.decision: Rebuild TUI from 6 screens to 3: PatternsScreen (1), BatchScreen (2), SettingsScreen (3) with number-key navigation
payload.rationale: Old TUI had confusing letter mnemonics (b/e/g/c/t), overlapping screens (Registry vs Generation vs Browser vs Dashboard). New structure has clear workflow: Patterns is the hub for all pattern work with ContentSwitcher for detail/generate modes.
alternatives_rejected: Keep 6 screens with letter mnemonics
